file_title: Optimizing CRM for Virtual Healthcare Growth
introduction: To continue our trajectory of growth, we need to leverage customer relations management software effectively. This presentation highlights key features that a CRM should possess to align with our virtual healthcare services.

subsections.heading: Understanding MediConn's Customer Engagement Needs
subsections.content:
• Recognizing the legal compliance significance of CRM systems in line with Canadian regulations established in July 2014
• Ensuring patient data is securely housed within Canadian borders for utmost privacy protection
• Providing bilingual information output in both French and English to cater to Canada's diverse patient population
• Leveraging CRM systems with advanced analytics capabilities to drive intelligent business decisions
• Employing automatic marketing communications features within the CRM system to improve patient engagement
• Integrating our CRM system with other healthcare systems to improve efficiency and patient care delivery
• Exploring the potential of AI to enhance customized patient communication and predict healthcare trends
subsections.justification: This content incorporates MediConn's unique CRM requirements and potential CRM features, aiding in answering the DR Question and specific question.

subsections.heading: Key Features of an Effective Healthcare CRM
subsections.content:
• CRM systems should be HIPAA compliant to protect patient data
• Omni-channel support, including email, phone, and chat, is critical for effective patient communication
• Integration with electronic health records (EHRs) enables seamless patient management
• CRM systems should have a user-friendly interface for ease of use
• Advanced security features are necessary to prevent data breaches
• It is important for CRM systems to have scalable architecture to support business growth
subsections.justification: The slide discusses general features of healthcare CRM systems, but does not specify what MediConn in particular would need in a CRM system.

subsections.heading: Integrating CRM into MediConn's Sales Strategy
subsections.content:
• MediConn's sales team has 50 members, each handling an average of 100 leads
• In 2025, 30% of leads were converted to paying customers
• The average lifetime value (LTV) of a MediConn customer is $500
• The sales team uses a 7-step sales process, from lead generation to closing
• In 2025, the average sales cycle was 45 days
• The sales team met their quarterly targets 80% of the time in 2025
subsections.justification: The slide focuses on MediConn's sales metrics and processes, but does not mention how a CRM system could contribute to future growth.

conclusion: By selecting a CRM that aligns with our unique needs and integrating it into our sales strategy, we can enhance customer engagement and drive growth. Let's explore potential vendors and prepare an implementation roadmap.
